body: WARRANT#84 shipped -> SLS(89)/10.03. Completed Keys-3 item 15's last piece: ShouldExitOnDissipation() closes an open trade when the readability episode transitions to DISSIPATING, wired into the existing trade-management loop right after the regime-change exit check. Gated HARD OFF by default -- same risk tier as sign-agnostic entries/soft-membership fitting/bracket-fallback, since closing a trade is a live-decision change. Cost-audited and cached per-bar (Keys3_Readability is expensive; without caching, N open trades per tick would each redundantly recompute it) -- same static-Time[0] idiom already used elsewhere in this file. Self-caught a real compile error mid-pass: first placement called two #define macros before their point of definition (macros, unlike functions, can't be forward-declared in MQL4) -- relocated the function body to after the macros' definition and used a plain function forward-declaration instead, recompiled clean. Diff-verified purely additive (one cosmetic whitespace line aside). Deployed, ledger at pass_69. Four warrants shipped this session now (#80-84 -- 81 was diagnostic-only, so 80/82/83/84 are the count of behavior-affecting ones), all gated or purely additive, all still unvalidated against a real run. Source, ex4, ledger attached.
